winner: I had to put down my favorite cat, Mr. Whiskers, but at least he went out with a drill in his paw and a book in his mouth—turns out he was secretly a tiny, furry construction worker with an insatiable thirst for knowledge.

candidates.text: I was so bad at using a drill that I thought it was just a book with a bunch of holes already punched in it!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative comparison between a drill and a book, with a surprising twist that is both specific and concise."}

candidates.text: I was trying to figure out how to use my new drill, but it kept getting stuck in this weird book. Turns out it wasn't a book at all—it was just a very stubborn piece of wood!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and uses both required words naturally. The twist of the 'book' being wood adds a clever, specific punchline."}

candidates.text: I was reading a book about how to be productive, and it said I should use a drill to make time for myself. I thought that was a great idea—until I realized I didn't have any wood to work with.
